messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Skills contributed by VS Code Client do not contain descriptions ISSUE BODY: The client customizations sent from client to agent host are missing the skill descriptions. See below. --- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # [Agent Skills (SKILL.md)](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/customization/agent-skills) Folders of instructions, scripts, and resources that agents load on-demand for specialized tasks. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Instructions 1. **Always read the `sessions` skill first.** This is your primary source of truth for the sessions architecture. - Invoke `skill: "sessions"`. 2. Focus your work on `src/vs/sessions/`. 3. Avoid making changes to core VS Code files (`src/vs/workbench/`, `src/vs/platform/`, etc.) unless absolutely necessary for the agents window functionality. ===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\.github\instructions\agentHostTesting.instructions.md ===== --- description: Architecture documentation for VS Code AI Customization view. Use when working in `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization` applyTo: 'src/vs/platform/agentHost/**' --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## Iterate 1. Draft the skill and save it. 2. Identify the most ambiguous or weak parts and ask about those. 3. Once finalized, summarize what the skill produces, suggest example prompts to try it, and propose related customizations to create next. Remember to follow the `agent-customization` guidelines to create highly effective skills. ===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\extensions\copilot\assets\prompts\skills\get-search-view-results\SKILL.md ===== --- name: get-search-view-results description: 'Get the current search results from the Search view in VS Code' --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## Anti-patterns - **Vague descriptions**: "A helpful skill" doesn't enable discovery - **Monolithic SKILL.md**: Everything in one file instead of references - **Name mismatch**: Folder name doesn't match `name` field - **Missing procedures**: Descriptions without step-by-step guidance ===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\extensions\copilot\assets\prompts\skills\agent-customization\SKILL.md ===== --- name: agent-customization user-invocable: false # don't show as slash command, we have sepcialized create-agent, create-instructions, create-hook prompts for that description: '**WORKFLOW SKILL** — Create, update, review, fix, or debug VS Code agent customization files (.instructions.md, .prompt.md, .agent.md, SKILL.md, copilot-instructions.md, AGENTS.md). USE FOR: saving coding preferences; troubleshooting why instructions/skills/agents are ignored or not invoked; configuring applyTo patterns; defining tool restrictions; creating custom agent modes or specialized workflows; packaging domain knowledge; fixing YAML frontmatter syntax. DO NOT USE FOR: general coding questions (use default agent); runtime debugging or error diagnosis; MCP server configuration (use MCP docs directly); VS Code extension development. INVOKES: file system tools (read/write customization files), ask-questions tool (interview user for requirements), subagents for codebase exploration. FOR SINGLE OPERATIONS: For quick YAML frontmatter fixes or creating a single file from a known pattern, edit the file directly — no skill needed.' --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # VS Code Agents Instructions This file provides instructions for AI coding agents working with the VS Code codebase. For detailed project overview, architecture, coding guidelines, and validation steps, see the [Copilot Instructions](.github/copilot-instructions.md).

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Fix: Skills contributed by VS Code Client do not contain descriptions",
  "story_type": "software_feature",
  "jira_issue_type": "Bug",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "copilot", "agent-skills"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a VS Code Copilot user, I want skill descriptions to be included when customizations are sent from the client to the agent host so that agents can properly discover and understand available skills.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given a SKILL.md file with a description field in YAML frontmatter, When the skill is contributed by the VS Code client to the agent host, Then the description should be included in the transmitted data",
    "Given an existing skill without a description, When it is updated via the UI, Then the description field should be populated and sent to the agent host",
    "Given the agent host receives skill customizations, Then each skill object must contain both 'name' and 'description' fields",
    "Given a user views skills in the AI Customization view, Then descriptions should be displayed alongside skill names"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Investigate how client customizations are serialized/sent to agent host",
      "Identify where skill metadata (name, description) is extracted from SKILL.md files",
      "Fix the serialization/transformation logic to include description field",
      "Add validation to ensure required fields (name, description) are present in skill objects before transmission"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Update AI Customization view UI to display skill descriptions",
      "Ensure description editing functionality works for skills without existing descriptions",
      "Verify description is properly saved and persisted when user updates a skill"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test that skill descriptions are transmitted from client to agent host",
      "Verify descriptions appear correctly in the AI Customization view UI",
      "Confirm skills with missing descriptions can be edited and descriptions are saved",
      "Validate that description field is included in all skill payloads sent to agent host"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Code reviewed by team lead",
    "All unit tests passing for skill serialization logic",
    "Integration tests verify client-to-agent-host data flow includes descriptions",
    "UI tests confirm descriptions display correctly in AI Customization view",
    "Documentation updated to reflect proper SKILL.md structure requirements"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "What is the exact data format/structure used when skills are sent from client to agent host?",
    "Are there any existing tests that verify skill metadata transmission between client and agent host?",
    "Is this a regression or a new issue - were descriptions previously included?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The SKILL.md file format with YAML frontmatter (name, description fields) is the source of truth for skill metadata",
    "There is an existing mechanism to serialize/send customizations from client extension to agent host",
    "The issue is specifically about the 'description' field being omitted during this transmission"
  ],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent Skills documentation",
    "D:\\VSss-codedata\\vscode\\.github\\instructions\\agentHostTesting.instructions.md - Architecture docs",
    "D:\\VSss-codedata\\vscode\\extensions\\copilot\\assets\\prompts\\skills\\get-search-view-results\\SKILL.md - Skill example",
    "D:\\VSss-codedata\\vscode\\extensions\\copilot\\assets\\prompts\\skills\\agent-customization\\SKILL.md - Agent customization skill"
  ]
}
```
